blocks.text: Attendees: Alice (TL), Bob (PM), Carol (infra), Dave (staff eng), Erin (SRE).
Goal: migrate the existing Python ingestion monolith to a set of Go microservices over the next two quarters, without regressing on end-to-end latency (p95 < 800ms).
Scope: ingest-api, dedup-worker, routing-service. Downstream consumers stay on the existing Kafka topics.
Non-goals: replacing the object-storage layer, moving off Postgres, rewriting the Python CLI.
Timeline: alpha by end of March, beta cut in mid-April, GA by mid-May.

Risks flagged: team has limited Go experience (mitigation: pair with platform team for first month); existing Python monolith carries undocumented behavior (mitigation: shadow-traffic mirror during beta).

Initial proposal — observability ownership: Erin owns building all GA dashboards single-handed; she's our SRE so the dashboard suite naturally falls to her. (Cross-doc anchor: this proposal is later revisited in p-005 and superseded in p-007.)
Initial proposal — dedup window: a 10-minute LRU window should be enough given current re-delivery patterns. (Cross-doc anchor: revised in p-004 design draft and finalized in p-007.)
Weekly Sync — 2026-02-17
Attendees: Alice, Bob, Carol, Dave.
Agenda: queue choice, service boundaries, staffing for sprint 1.
Decision: adopt Kafka as the event backbone for the new pipeline (vs. NATS or RabbitMQ) — aligns with existing downstream consumers and avoids a second operational surface.
Decision: split ingest-api and dedup-worker into separate services from day 1, even though it's tempting to start with a monolith.

Initial proposal — schema evolution: lean toward JSON-Schema for the on-wire event envelope; the team is already using JSON elsewhere, easier to debug. Marked tentative pending Carol's benchmark. (Cross-doc anchor: re-opened on p-005, finalized differently on p-008.)
Action items: Carol to spike Kafka client library choice by 2026-02-24; Dave to draft the ingest-api service contract.
Weekly Sync — 2026-02-24
Attendees: Alice, Bob, Dave, Erin.
Carol's Kafka spike: recommend franz-go over confluent-kafka-go (better context.Context support, fewer cgo headaches).
Decision: use franz-go.
Weekly Sync — 2026-03-03
Attendees: Alice, Bob, Carol, Dave, Erin.
Prototype ingest-api hitting 1.2k RPS on a single pod in staging — headroom to 2x with the current CPU request.
Decision: use Go (golang 1.23) as the primary language for ingester and all downstream services in this workstream.
Decision: deploy as a statefulset with local SSD for the dedup LRU cache (alternatives: Redis sidecar, in-memory only) — picked statefulset for cost + latency.
Weekly Sync — 2026-03-17
Attendees: Alice, Bob, Carol, Dave.
Alpha rollout is a week behind; two load-test crashes tied to the franz-go batch reclaim path.
Action item: Dave to open upstream issue + work around with manual flush; ship alpha 2026-03-25.
Staffing: Erin rotating off for two weeks, Priya backfilling.
Design Doc — Ingester — v2 draft
Author: Dave. Reviewers: Alice, Carol.
Goal: accept up to 10k events/sec per region, dedup on (tenant_id, event_id), route to the right downstream topic.
Architecture: HTTP/2 ingress behind an Envoy gateway → ingest-api (stateless) → dedup-worker (statefulset with a bounded LRU, 15-minute window) → router → Kafka.

Revision — dedup window: superseding the 10-minute proposal from kickoff (p-001), this draft adopts a 15-minute LRU window after observing the alpha re-delivery tail. Still subject to retro feedback once beta data is in. (Cross-doc anchor: this 15-minute number is itself superseded in p-007.)
Backpressure: ingest-api returns 429 when dedup-worker lag > 5s, gateway retries with jitter.
Decision: reject in-flight events older than the dedup window instead of silently letting duplicates through — safer default, callers already retry with their own idempotency keys.
Open questions see page p-005.
Open Questions
Backpressure policy: should we drop or retry on sustained overload? Current prototype retries up to 3×, but that masks real capacity issues.

Schema evolution revisit: p-002's tentative JSON-Schema lean is no longer the working assumption. Carol's benchmark shows protobuf wins on payload size and decode speed; final call deferred to a beta retro. (Cross-doc anchor: confirmed on p-008.)
Multi-region dedup: do we dedup per-region only, or is there a global guarantee we need? Currently per-region.

Observability ownership revisit: p-001 said Erin alone owns dashboards; the alpha showed that's not realistic given her on-call load. Pending decision on whether to share ownership. (Cross-doc anchor: resolved on p-007.)
Runtime config: Feature flags via GrowthBook vs. env-vars-only — no decision yet.
References
Internal: the existing Python monolith lives in github.com/internal/ingestor-legacy; architecture notes in that repo's docs/ directory.
Retro — Alpha (2026-03-31)
What went well
Alpha shipped only one week late, despite the franz-go flush bug.
Load tests caught the statefulset eviction issue before any customer traffic.
Pairing with platform team for Go reviews unblocked us multiple times.
What to improve
We underestimated operational readiness — SRE asked for dashboards we hadn't built.
Design reviews ran too long (2+ hrs) because the design doc was still in draft during review.
Decisions

Final decision — observability ownership: supersedes p-001 (Erin solo) and the p-005 open question. Dave and Erin co-own the GA dashboard set; Erin keeps SLO/error-budget dashboards, Dave owns ingest-pipeline saturation and franz-go internals. This is the working ownership model going into GA.

Final decision — dedup window size: supersedes p-001 (10 min) and p-004 (15 min). Adopt a 20-minute LRU window for GA based on the alpha re-delivery distribution; the 15-minute draft cut off ~3% of legitimate retries. The 20-minute number is the binding GA value.
Actions
Dave to partner with Erin on the GA dashboard set by 2026-04-14.
Alice to enforce the 'design doc fully written before review' rule for beta.
Retro — Beta (2026-04-18)
What went well
Beta hit the performance target (p95 ingest latency: 710ms under 2x load).
Shadow-traffic diff against legacy Python ingester found only 0.03% mismatch — mostly timestamp rounding.
What to improve
The dedup LRU needs a proper persistence story before GA (pod restarts lose ~20 min of dedup state under the new window).
Runbook still incomplete — SRE escalated once during the first weekend.
Decisions

Final decision — schema evolution: supersedes p-002 (tentative JSON-Schema) and resolves the p-005 open question. Adopt protobuf for the on-wire event envelope. Carol's beta benchmark showed ~38% smaller payloads and ~2.4x faster decode versus JSON-Schema; tooling cost (codegen) is a one-time investment. This is the binding GA decision.
Action items
Carol to propose a snapshot-to-S3 approach for dedup LRU by 2026-04-28.
Dave + Erin to close out the runbook before GA.
